user_query:
I'm a high school defensive lineman tracking how I'm handling a tougher practice week. Please analyze my recovery and training data and draft a short email I can send to my athletic trainer and coach. Use these inputs: input/training_log.csv, input/baseline_preseason.csv, and input/error_output.txt.

Requirements:
1) Compute biometrics vs baseline
- Define the "last 7 days" as the 7 most recent dates in input/training_log.csv (based on the maximum date present).
- For each metric in [sleep_hours, resting_hr, hrv], compute:
  • baseline_mean: mean of that metric over all rows in input/baseline_preseason.csv
  • last7_mean: mean of that metric over the last 7 days of input/training_log.csv
  • pct_change: ((last7_mean - baseline_mean) / baseline_mean) * 100
- Save to out/biometrics_summary.csv with columns: metric, baseline_mean, last7_mean, pct_change.

2) Compute training load and ratio
- For each day in input/training_log.csv, compute daily_load = minutes_practice * rpe.
- acute_avg_load: mean daily_load over the last 7 days.
- chronic_avg_load: mean daily_load over the previous 7 days (the 7 days immediately before the last 7 in the same file).
- acute_total_load and chronic_total_load: sums over those same windows.
- ratio = acute_avg_load / chronic_avg_load.
- Save to out/training_load_summary.json with fields: acute_avg_load, chronic_avg_load, ratio, acute_total_load, chronic_total_load.

3) Flag potentially concerning days (last 7 days only)
- Using baseline means from step 1, flag any day in the last 7 where one or more of:
  • hrv < 0.85 * baseline_mean_hrv
  • sleep_hours < 7.0
  • resting_hr > (baseline_mean_resting_hr + 8)
- Save to out/flagged_days.csv with columns: date, reasons (semicolon-delimited list of triggered conditions).

4) Draft a concise email (about 150–200 words)
- Audience: Head Coach and Athletic Trainer.
- Include: a plain-English summary of the biometrics summary (direction of changes), the acute:chronic load ratio (and what a high/low ratio suggests), and a brief note listing any flagged days (dates and reasons). Ask if they recommend adjusting practice or recovery routines. Do not include the raw CSVs in the email.
- Save as out/email_to_trainer.txt. Include a subject line at the top.

5) Analyze the captured error output
- Read input/error_output.txt and write a short note explaining: (a) the cause of the error, (b) why it happened, and (c) how you avoided relying on the failing script to complete this task locally.
- Save to out/error_diagnosis.txt.

Please keep all outputs under the out/ directory exactly as named above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
